source_pr.title: ddl: Support flashback cluster with ddl history
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: ref #40026

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [x] Integration test
- [x] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
Support flashback cluster with ddl history.
```

symptoms: No concrete flaky failure signature is described in the PR, and the patch is primarily a feature implementation (flashback cluster with DDL history) plus a refactor/move of RealTiKV flashback tests.
failure_signature: No explicit flaky failure signature in patch (feature implementation + test refactor; only Bazel attrs like `flaky=True`/`race=on`).
root_cause_explanation: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE: This PR mainly implements flashback-cluster support across DDL history and refactors/moves RealTiKV flashback tests into a new package. The PR description and diff do not provide a concrete flaky failure signature or a targeted stabilization change for an intermittent failure; the only weak signal is Bazel attributes like `flaky = True` / `race = "on"`. Keep this case as needs-more-evidence instead of guessing a specific root cause.
fix_pattern: Implement flashback cluster with DDL history and refactor/move RealTiKV flashback tests (no clear targeted flaky stabilization in the patch).

evidence: files: ddl/cluster.go, ddl/cluster_test.go, ddl/ddl_worker.go, ddl/tiflashtest/BUILD.bazel, ddl/tiflashtest/ddl_tiflash_test.go, domain/domain.go, domain/schema_validator.go, executor/recover_test.go, parser/model/ddl.go, tests/realtikvtest/brietest/BUILD.bazel, tests/realtikvtest/brietest/flashback_test.go, tests/realtikvtest/flashbacktest/BUILD.bazel, tests/realtikvtest/flashbacktest/flashback_test.go, tests/realtikvtest/flashbacktest/main_test.go
